heading: Alternatives to payment bonds provided by Federal Acquisition Regulation
label: 3132 Alternatives to payment bonds provided by Federal Acquisition Regulation
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)313240:270a note., , .Pub. L. 103–355, title IV, § 4104(b)(2)108 Stat. 3342Oct. 13, 1994
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)313240:270a note., , .Pub. L. 103–355, title IV, § 4104(b)(2)108 Stat. 3342Oct. 13, 1994

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Federal Acquisition Regulation shall provide alternatives to payment bonds as payment protections for suppliers of labor and materials under contracts referred to in that are more than $25,000 and not more than $100,000.section 3131(a) of this title

select, from among the payment protections provided for in the Federal Acquisition Regulation pursuant to subsection (a), one or more payment protections which the offeror awarded the contract is to submit to the Federal Government for the protection of suppliers of labor and materials for the contract; and
specify in the solicitation of offers for the contract the payment protections selected.
